summary: End-to-end testing of Medlems- og Arrangementsapp completed. 13/13 backend pytest cases pass. All frontend flows (admin login, dashboard stats, members search wildcard, Excel idempotent re-import, event CRUD, participant add/edit/remove, user creation, non-admin read-only role enforcement) verified through Playwright. One real UI bug found: Add-Participant dialog does not auto-close after confirm-add.

ui_bugs.issue: After clicking 'participant-confirm-add' the dialog stays open instead of auto-closing. Participant is correctly added in background, but user must manually press Esc or X. Confirmed by data-testid='add-participant-dialog' count still being 1 (>0) after confirm. This blocks repeated additions and makes UI feel stuck.
Console warning: 'Missing Description or aria-describedby={undefined} for {DialogContent}'. Affects Add Participant and Create Event/User dialogs. Accessibility-only.

action_items: Fix EventDetailPage: call setDialogOpen(false) (or reset state) after successful POST /api/events/{id}/participants so the dialog auto-closes. Also clear participant-search-input + participant-note-input on close.
Add aria-describedby (or <DialogDescription>) to each shadcn DialogContent to remove the a11y console warnings.
Optional: include a 'Description or aria-describedby' on the create-event, add-participant, create-user dialogs.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py:255 cookie set with samesite='none' + secure=True is correct for cross-site preview, but won't work over plain http locally — acceptable since production uses HTTPS.
server.py:583 CORS uses allow_origin_regex='.*' with allow_credentials=True. Works in preview but should be restricted to a CORS_ORIGINS allowlist for production hardening.
No brute-force / rate limiting on /api/auth/login. Not blocking, but worth a follow-up for production hardening.
Excel import endpoint loads entire file into memory and iterates synchronously inside an async handler — fine for ~600 rows but could be batched (bulk_write) for larger files.
get_member uses ObjectId(member_id) without try/except — malformed IDs will return 500 instead of 404. Same for /events/{id} and participant endpoints.
Member listing relies on case-insensitive regex on every search; for larger datasets consider a MongoDB text index on (medlemsnummer, navn, adresse, telefon, email).
test_credentials: Admin: tdeele@gmail.com / Fransen123!!! ; Non-admin created during testing: testuser_qa@example.com / Test1234! (created via API in backend tests, also exercised in UI)
seed_data_creation: Created non-admin user testuser_qa@example.com (role=user) and one event 'QA Auto Event' on 2026-07-01 in København. Two participants currently attached (Thorbjørn Deele + Annette J. B. Hansen). Also created a few UI test users (uitest_<timestamp>@example.com) via /brugere. Excel re-import already verified (inserted=0, updated=589).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend is fully green. Only outstanding UI item is the add-participant dialog not auto-closing after 'participant-confirm-add'. After E1 patches EventDetailPage to close & reset the dialog post-add, re-run Playwright add-participant flow twice in succession to confirm the dialog closes on its own. 589 members are seeded; 'QA Auto Event' exists with leftover test participants — feel free to clean up via DELETE /api/events/{id}.
rca of the issue: Add Participant dialog stays open after confirm: EventDetailPage's confirm handler likely awaits the POST but does not call setOpen(false) / does not reset the controlled Dialog state on success. Reproduction: open event detail → click 'Tilføj deltager' → search → pick member → 'Bekræft' → observe dialog remains visible while the new participant appears in the table. Mitigation: in the onConfirm handler, after successful axios.post resolve, call setDialogOpen(false), clear searchQuery, selectedMember, and note state.
